tasks.description: Extract playground-local state and panel models out of `_PlaygroundPage.tsx` into reusable, testable state modules while preserving existing A/B streaming, skills, prompt, grading, and tool flows.
steps: `packages/kumo-docs-astro/src/lib/playground/types.ts` exists with shared panel/layout/editor state types used by the playground UI
`packages/kumo-docs-astro/src/lib/playground/state.ts` exists with pure reducer/helpers for panel updates, editor updates, and layout updates
`packages/kumo-docs-astro/src/components/demos/_PlaygroundPage.tsx` no longer stores mirrored `foo` / `noPromptFoo` state inline for core panel artifacts
Existing A/B generation still works for both panels with current abort semantics unchanged
Existing features still work after extraction: skill apply on panel B, tool confirmation cards, prompt tab, grading tab, and action log

tasks.description: Add a `Tree` tab that renders a human-readable nested representation of the active panel `UITree` and keeps it live during streaming.
steps: Panel tabs include `Tree` for both panel A and panel B
`Tree` view shows stable element key, type, props, action metadata, and recursively nested children
`Tree` view updates while a stream is in progress for the active panel
`Tree` view supports copy-to-clipboard of the rendered nested representation
Empty state is shown when the active panel has no renderable tree

tasks.description: Add a safe editable JSON `Editor` tab for local panel tree edits with formatting, validation, apply, and reset flows.
steps: Panel tabs include `Editor` for both panel A and panel B
`packages/kumo-docs-astro/src/lib/playground/validate-tree.ts` exists and validates edited JSON text before any apply
Invalid JSON and invalid `UITree` shape produce structured validation issues and never replace the last valid rendered tree
`Editor` exposes `Format`, `Validate`, `Apply`, and `Reset to latest streamed tree` controls
Applying valid JSON updates only the active panel's local tree and derived views (preview, TSX, grading, tree) without mutating chat history or triggering a new API request
Reset restores the latest streamed tree for the active panel

tasks.description: Create shared catalog explorer data for playground components and actions using registry metadata and explicit action definitions.
steps: `packages/kumo-docs-astro/src/lib/playground/catalog-data.ts` exists and exports normalized component and action entries for the playground
Component metadata is sourced from `virtual:kumo-registry` rather than duplicated manually
Action metadata includes built-in actions `increment`, `decrement`, `reset`, `set`, `toggle`, `submit_form`, and `navigate`
Action metadata also includes playground-specific actions `tool_approve` and `tool_cancel`
Catalog data exposes names, descriptions, and parameter metadata needed by the UI explorer

tasks.description: Add a shared catalog explorer that is accessible from desktop and mobile without duplicating catalog tabs inside both A/B panels.
steps: Desktop playground exposes a shared catalog drawer or sheet from the workspace header
Mobile playground exposes `Catalog` as a first-class top-level view
Catalog explorer supports search by component/action name
Catalog explorer supports component category filtering
Component entries show description, import path or docs hint, and prop names/types/requiredness
Action entries show description and parameter names/types
Catalog explorer is usable without leaving `/playground`

tasks.description: Upgrade the existing code view into an explicit TSX export surface with copy and download actions.
steps: Panel tab label is `TSX` instead of `Code`
TSX tab still uses `uiTreeToJsx()` output from the active panel tree
TSX tab provides a copy action for the full generated `.tsx` module
TSX tab provides a download action that saves a `.tsx` file locally
Downloaded/exported component names default to `GeneratedPanelA` and `GeneratedPanelB` for the respective panels
If export output includes playground-only components such as `DemoButton`, the UI shows an explicit warning

tasks.description: Replace the fixed desktop shell with a resizable workspace that supports both chat resizing and A/B panel resizing.
steps: Desktop layout uses resizable panels for `chat <-> workspace` and nested `panel A <-> panel B`
Chat minimize behavior still works inside the resizable shell
Resizable sizes persist across refresh in the same browser via local storage or equivalent client persistence
Existing panel content (preview, jsonl, grading, prompt, actions, skills) still renders correctly inside the resizable layout
No route changes are introduced; `/playground` remains the main page and `/playground/report` still works

tasks.description: Add a mobile-first single-column shell that can switch between chat, panel A, panel B, and catalog views without losing state.
steps: At mobile widths, the playground renders a single-column shell instead of side-by-side A/B columns
Mobile shell exposes top-level view switching for `Chat`, `A`, `B`, and `Catalog`
Each panel retains its own active tab and local state when switching mobile views
Chat state and messages are preserved while switching between mobile views
Catalog remains reachable on mobile without leaving `/playground`

tasks.description: Add regression coverage for extracted state, tree inspection, editor apply/reset flows, catalog explorer data, and layout persistence.
steps: Unit tests cover playground state transitions for tab changes, editor dirty/apply/reset flows, and layout persistence payloads
Unit tests cover nested tree transformation for representative `UITree` fixtures
Unit tests cover tree validation for valid JSON, malformed JSON, and invalid `UITree` shapes
Integration tests cover stream -> edit JSON -> apply -> preview/TSX/grading update for a panel
Regression coverage verifies existing playground flows still work: A/B streaming, panel B skill apply, tool confirmation cards, prompt view, and action log

patterns: Branch rule: before starting any implementation task, verify the current branch is `geoquant/streaming-ui`; do not start new work on any other branch.
Keep route structure stable: `/playground` stays the main route and `/playground/report` stays intact.
Preserve the existing A/B comparison architecture instead of collapsing the playground into a single-output workflow.
Use `streamJsonlUI` + `useUITree` + `UITreeRenderer` as the streaming/rendering pipeline; do not invent a second tree runtime.
Use `uiTreeToJsx()` for generated TSX rather than bespoke codegen.
Validate edited trees at the boundary using Kumo catalog validation; do not use unchecked casts to treat arbitrary JSON as `UITree`.
Prefer extracting reusable playground helpers under `src/lib/playground/` over growing `_PlaygroundPage.tsx` further.
Catalog explorer should reuse registry metadata patterns from `RegistryDemo.tsx` rather than duplicating registry knowledge manually.
nonGoals: Version history or iterative snapshots in v1
Server-side persistence, saved sessions, or share URLs
Automatically feeding manual JSON edits back into chat history or the next model request
Replacing the current tool confirmation flow, skills flow, grading flow, or eval report flow
Introducing a node-graph editor or third-party visual JSON editor as part of this spec
